mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60 days in >50% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration coincides with negative 5-day SPX returns in >65% of observations, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation surpassing 3.0% forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, raising real discount rates and triggering equity market sell-offs that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >55% of months following CPI breaks above 3.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for Fed funds rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and wage growth, which increases household disposable income and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede market declines.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized strength in durable goods demand and consumer confidence, which sustains earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices signals supply-side constraints and stagflationary pressures, which compress profit margins and trigger rapid equity liquidation over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >50% of periods where industrial production falls while CPI rises, or the mechanism fails to predict returns during historical stagflation episodes.
